data.description:
# Eliminate canonical-vs-display form divergence: single display-form throughout

## The bug, demonstrated live

Two real symptoms in the running app on 2026-05-02 (screenshots in
the originating conversation):

### Codex case
- Beats table row: Agent="Codex" Model="gpt" Version="5.5"
- Same agent's title-bar pill: "GPT 5.5"
- User's expectation: "Codex GPT 5.5" — and the table column should
  read "GPT" not "gpt"

### OpenCode case
- Title-bar pill: `OpenCode openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 openrouter 2.6`
- User's expectation: `OpenCode OpenRouter MoonshotAI Kimi-k 2.6`

## Why this isn't a "display layer" bug

The user correctly pointed out: the only way "gpt" appears in a
display surface is if the display formatter applies `.toLowerCase()`.
It does not — `formatModelDisplay` capitalises via `MODEL_LABELS`.

The lowercase "gpt" is the actual stored data. The bug is upstream:
the codebase carries TWO parallel forms of every model name:

- **Machine token** (lowercase canonical): `"gpt"`, `"claude"`,
  `"opus"`. What `normalizeCodexModel` / `normalizeClaudeModel` /
  etc. emit. What we stamp onto the lease via `lease_model`.
- **Display token**: `"GPT"`, `"Claude"`, `"Opus"`. What
  `formatModelDisplay` produces via the `MODEL_LABELS` lookup.

Some readers go through `formatModelDisplay` (title bar pill);
others read the stamped field directly (beats table Model column).
Result: two renderings of the same data. That is precisely the
duplication / divergence the canonical contract was meant to
eliminate. We achieved single-extraction (foolery-2e97 children) but
not single-form.

The OpenCode case is the same disease in a different shape:
`normalizeOpenCodeModel` stores BOTH `model = "openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6"`
AND `flavor = "openrouter"`. The flavor is a substring of the model.
Display concatenates them, producing the duplicated "openrouter 2.6"
tail.

## The fix

**Single canonical form = display form. No translation step.** The
per-provider extractors emit display-correct strings. The lease
stamps display-correct strings. Every reader renders the field
as-is. `MODEL_LABELS` and `formatModelDisplay` either disappear or
become identity passes.

Specifically:
1. `normalizeClaudeModel` returns `model: "Claude"`, `flavor: "Opus"`
   (or `"Sonnet"` / `"Haiku"`), `version: "4.7"` — NOT
   `"claude"`, `"opus"`.
2. `normalizeCodexModel` returns `model: "GPT"` / `"ChatGPT"`,
   `flavor: "Codex"` / `"Codex Mini"` / etc., `version: "5.5"`.
3. `normalizeGeminiModel` returns `model: "Gemini"`,
   `flavor: "Pro"` / `"Flash"` / `"Flash Lite"`, `version: "2.5"`.
4. `normalizeCopilotModel` keeps its current "delegate to inner
   provider" logic but the inner provider's display-form propagates.
5. `normalizeOpenCodeModel` is reshaped to produce a clean display
   label. Options for the data shape:
   - (preferred) `model: "OpenRouter MoonshotAI Kimi-k"`,
     `flavor` undefined, `version: "2.6"` — pre-formatted display
     string with proper capitalisation.
   - OR keep the path in a separate field for routing decisions, but
     `model` carries the display string.
6. `formatAgentFamily` and `formatAgentOptionLabel` simplify to
   `[provider, model, flavor, version].filter(Boolean).join(" ")`
   with no per-provider special cases, no `MODEL_LABELS` lookup, no
   `formatModelDisplay` call.
7. The lease's `agent_info.model` carries the display form.
   Downstream readers (beat table, pills, audit) render it as-is.
8. `MODEL_LABELS` is either removed entirely or repurposed only for
   one-time legacy-data migration.
9. The provider order in `formatAgentFamily` for Codex specifically:
   include the provider — "Codex GPT 5.5", not "GPT 5.5". The
   current code drops "Codex" when model is "GPT"; that's the
   wrong call given the user's stated expectation.

## OpenCode segment formatting

Path tokens need proper capitalisation:
- `openrouter` → `OpenRouter`
- `moonshotai` → `MoonshotAI` (compound; recognise common AI/ML
  vendor patterns: trailing "ai" → "AI", trailing "ml" → "ML"; or
  a small explicit vocabulary table)
- `kimi-k2.6` → `Kimi-k 2.6` (split version: regex on first numeric
  run after the model name)
- `claude-sonnet-4-5` (in OpenRouter path) → `Claude Sonnet 4.5`
- `glm-5.1` → `Glm 5.1`
- `devstral-2512` → `Devstral 2512`
- Path is `<router>/<vendor>/<model-with-version>` — three segments
  formatted with appropriate capitalisation rules

A small explicit vocabulary table for known compound vendor names
(`moonshotai → MoonshotAI`, `openrouter → OpenRouter`, etc.) is
acceptable. Auto-capitalisation via "trailing AI/ML/IO/JS becomes
upper" can supplement.

## Migration

Existing on-disk data carries machine-form values:
- `settings.toml` agent entries from foolery-c4a6's canonicalisation
- step-history records on already-shipped knots
- Active leases (if any)

For settings: write-time migration on first read (same idempotent
pattern foolery-c4a6 introduced). Detect machine-form, convert via
the new extractor, persist.

For historical step records / notes / handoffs: leave them alone.
They're history. New entries written after this change ship in
display form. The beat-detail UI may show a mix of "gpt" (old
records) and "GPT" (new records); that's acceptable historical
artefact — do NOT retroactively rewrite the JSONL.

For leases active at deploy time: terminate or reconcile them
during the rollout; they're short-lived.

## Why the previous nine commits did not get this right

The contract focused on "one extraction site, one display function,
no fallbacks". It is silent on "what is the canonical FORM". The
codebase had two forms before the work and still has two forms
after. The work centralised the extraction-call topology but kept
the dual-form data. That is what the user means by "failed to come
even remotely close to the purpose of the work". The purpose was
to eliminate divergence — and the divergence was always at the
form level, not the call-site level.

## Cross-references

- Parent: foolery-2e97
- Related shipped: foolery-988b (OpenCode extractor — incomplete in
  retrospect; it stored model + flavor that overlap)
- Contract: `docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md`
- Tag: `agent-normalizer-fucker`
